summary: Iteration 35 - Improved TV Dashboard with 3-column layout testing complete. Backend: 17/17 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI components verified. The improved TV Dashboard correctly displays: 3-column layout (Clients | Offline+Incidents | Alerts+Toner), offline devices with pulsing red indicator and enriched data (name, IP, client, down_since), incidents with priority badges (P1/P2) and status (APERTO/IN CORSO), alerts with device info and time_ago, connectors with hostname/version/heartbeat, low toner with colored bars, and scrolling ticker with LIVE badge.

minor.issue: Some alerts show empty device_name/device_ip and client_name shows client_id instead of name - this is a DATA issue (alerts belong to non-existent clients), not a code bug

backend_api: Response has all required keys: timestamp, global_stats, clients, offline_devices, alerts, incidents, connectors, low_toner, ticker
global_stats has all 12 required metrics (total_clients, total_devices, total_online, total_offline, etc.)
offline_devices array has enriched fields: name, ip, client_name, down_since
alerts array has enriched fields: device_name, device_ip, client_name, time_ago
incidents array has fields: title, priority, status, client_name, time_ago
connectors array has fields: client_name, hostname, version, online, last_seen
ticker array has fields: severity, message, client_name, time_ago
low_toner array has fields: printer_name, printer_ip, client_name, supply_name, level_pct, color_hex
clients array has comprehensive summary with 14 fields including problem_devices
frontend_ui: Header shows '86BIT NOC Center' title with NOC logo
Global status badge shows 'ATTENZIONE' when offline devices exist
Stats bar shows 8 metrics: DISPOSITIVI, ONLINE, OFFLINE, CRITICI, ALERT, INCIDENTI, STAMPANTI, TONER
LEFT COLUMN: Client cards with health ring, online/offline counts, connector status
LEFT COLUMN: Connector chips with hostname, version, last_seen
CENTER COLUMN: Offline devices with pulsing red indicator, device name, IP, client name, down_since
CENTER COLUMN: Incidents with priority badges (P1/P2/P3/P4) and status (APERTO/IN CORSO)
RIGHT COLUMN: Alerts with severity label, device name, IP, client name, time_ago
RIGHT COLUMN: Low toner with colored progress bars and percentages
FOOTER: Ticker scrolling automatically with event messages

critical_code_review_comments: tv_dashboard.py: API correctly enriches offline_devices with name, ip, client_name, down_since
tv_dashboard.py: _time_ago() function correctly formats timestamps in Italian (s fa, m fa, h m fa, g fa)
tv_dashboard.py: Alerts enriched with device_name lookup from managed_devices collection
tv_dashboard.py: Incidents include priority and status fields for badge display
tv_dashboard.py: Connectors include hostname, version, online status, last_seen
TvDashboardPage.js: 3-column grid layout with proper responsive breakpoints
TvDashboardPage.js: Ticker auto-scrolls every 40ms with transform animation
TvDashboard.css: Proper CSS variables for consistent theming
TvDashboard.css: Pulsing animations for offline indicators and critical stats

test_credentials.note: TV Dashboard at /tv does NOT require authentication

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Using existing demo data: 13 devices (10 online, 3 offline), 8 alerts (4 critical), 2 incidents, 1 connector, 4 low toner items.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 35 verified the IMPROVED TV Dashboard with 3-column layout. All features working: 1) Header with 86BIT NOC Center title and real-time clock. 2) Global status badge (ATTENZIONE when problems exist). 3) Stats bar with 8 metrics. 4) LEFT column: Client cards with health ring + Connector chips. 5) CENTER column: Offline devices with pulsing red dot + Incidents with P1/P2 badges. 6) RIGHT column: Alerts with enriched device info + Low toner with colored bars. 7) Footer with LIVE badge and scrolling ticker. Minor data issue: some alerts have empty device info because they belong to non-existent clients (data cleanup needed, not code bug).

mocked_apis.firebase_push: Firebase Cloud Messaging (push notifications) is MOCKED
